mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% (near zero) signals an extended monetary easing regime that depresses discount rates on equity cash flows. This persistently low rate environment produces sustained positive S&P 500 returns as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with near-zero rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS at 147,553k) signals robust labor market strength and consumer income growth, which drives aggregate demand and corporate earnings. This positive labor momentum predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward-looking equity prices incorporate earnings expectations.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 50% of months when PAYEMS growth is positive, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 1,615k units) and retail sales (RSAFS at 625,416M) jointly signal consumer spending capacity and residential investment demand. When both series remain elevated, aggregate demand supports corporate profit margins, producing positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,615k and RSAFS at 625,416M as of February-March 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with sustained consumer demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when both HOUST and RSAFS are above their 12-month median, or the interaction term (HOUST × RSAFS) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 101.59) declines below its 3-month moving average, signaling weakening manufacturing activity and supply-side constraints. This supply weakness triggers equity market concerns about future earnings capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC of INDPRO momentum is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 274.14) rises above 2.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 2.0%, creating a negative real rate environment. This regime triggers expectations of future monetary tightening, causing equity valuations to compress and producing negative 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 274.14 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of March 2007
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with rising inflation and low rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (inflation × rate gap) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE at 4.8%) rises above 5.0% in a single month, signaling an abrupt deterioration in labor market conditions. This threshold crossing triggers equity market repricing of recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration signaling recession risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or Rank IC of UNRATE level is not significantly negative (p > 0.10) in the 5-day forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1 at 19,368.31B) decelerates below 2.0% annualized rate while housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,500k units, signaling a demand-side slowdown. This dual weakness triggers equity market concerns about earnings recession, producing negative 60-day returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized AND HOUST < 1,500k units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,368.31B as of Q4 2006 and HOUST at 1,615k as of February 2007
regime_hint: late-cycle demand deceleration with housing weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (GDP growth × housing starts) has Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and payroll employment (PAYEMS) both decline month-over-month simultaneously, signaling synchronized weakness in production capacity and labor utilization. This dual supply-side contraction predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as markets price in earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.59 and PAYEMS at 147,553k as of February-March 2007
regime_hint: synchronized supply-side contraction in late cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (INDPRO × PAYEMS momentum) has Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
